$comment: Index of reusable product patterns Chief Wiggum can stamp into the apps it builds. See docs/patterns-registry.md. Each entry points at patterns/<id>/{pattern.md,manifest.json}. Candidates are mined/known but not yet fully specified.
$model: A pattern IS a cluster of invariants that must hold together, plus the playbook to satisfy them. See docs/patterns-registry.md#patterns-as-clusters-of-invariants. Each pattern/candidate carries an `invariants` array of generic stable ids (INV-<ABBR>-NNN) with `realized_as` provenance (the mined app's app-specific id + code). /architect pulls the cluster into invariants.md by id so connected requirements are never re-derived; the traceability/single-writer/ratchet gates then hold the whole cluster.

patterns.title: Improvement Lifecycle Loop
patterns.summary: Autonomous, signal-driven, ratchet-gated fix-forward refinement installed into the product. Trusted-signal changes deploy autonomously; untrusted-signal (end-user) changes quarantine for blocking admin approval.

patterns.title: Engagement Instrumentation
patterns.summary: Server-trusted, tamper-resistant capture of how far users get through assigned work/content, robust to out-of-order/replay/concurrent/clock-drift. The instrumentation tier that feeds the improvement loop; each signal annotated with its trust class.

patterns.title: Tiered Subscription
patterns.summary: Subscribe -> enforce -> lifecycle machinery for a plan-tiered SaaS: single-source tier matrix, injected gate seams, stateless quota, billing-webhook as source of truth, and non-destructive graceful degradation on downgrade/lapse. Absorbs the former tiered-saas-enforcement candidate.

patterns.title: Frictionless Onboarding (free -> upgrade)
patterns.summary: Minimize time-to-value on a free tier, convert at contextual friction points (limit-hit prompts, optional reverse trial), self-serve. The mini-SaaS pattern most improved by the loop -- and, since its proposals touch pricing/paywall driven by end-user behavior, always admin-gated.

patterns.title: Deployment & Release
patterns.summary: build -> stage -> promote machinery that gets a change to prod safely and provably: keyless federated CI identity, environment split with a human-gated prod promotion, promotion of the byte-identical staged artifact, a config-leak fence, a least-privilege deploy identity that can roll releases but not mutate infra/read secrets, and post-deploy smoke verification. Mined from two shipped GCP apps.

patterns.title: Fetch-on-Webhook Reconcile (non-monotonic external state)
patterns.summary: Correct idempotency for a webhook whose external resource is NON-monotonic (subscriptions, refunds): event = trigger only, live fetch = truth, never copy mutable state off the payload. Order-immune, unknown-id-fatal (never silent-floor), retry-lease dedupe, terminal-deletion latch. Ships its sibling MONOTONIC-FSM branch (video-upload style) so the selection criterion travels with it. Expressed as an invariant cluster (INV-FOWR-*).

patterns.title: Revocable Elevated-Access Session (support impersonation)
patterns.summary: Time-boxed, revocable, dual-identity-audited operator act-as. Session TTL enforced against the session's own iat (independent of the IdP token life), future-skew guard, fail-closed revocation, no nested impersonation, boundary-checked deny-list (operator plane + auth-factor + destructive/GDPR), dual-identity audit before every impersonated mutation, un-forgeable at the signing root. Promoted from candidate; realized 1:1 in dogeared-coach as INV-EAS-001..007.

patterns.title: Multi-Tenant Isolation Stack
patterns.summary: The isolation floor: server-only tenant resolution (reject client hints, anti-probe 404) -> fail-closed tenant-scoped repository (no unscoped path, reject-don't-override recursively incl. $rename string values, unexported handles) -> standing cross-tenant proof gate (denial AND no-mutation AND no-foreign-id-leak) -> cancel-latched resumable transactional cascade erasure with a tax-tombstone exception. The operator plane sits OUTSIDE tenancy. Promoted from candidate; INV-MTI-001..006.

patterns.title: Provider-Neutral Port/Adapter
patterns.summary: The swappability seam for an external vendor: single-file vendor confinement (SDK imported in exactly one file, import-lint enforceable), neutral DTOs only + unexported concrete adapter with a compile-time var _ Port assertion (+ Fake), vendor-state->enum with safe defaults, signed-webhook-normalized-to-neutral-event (webhook is truth, not the client redirect), sign-per-request TTL-clamped tokens, and direct browser->CDN upload via a one-time ticket (API credential never reaches the browser). Promoted from candidate; realized twice in dogeared-coach (video + billing). INV-PNA-001..006.

stacks.$comment: Concrete stack profiles bind these vendor-neutral patterns to a real infrastructure stack + runnable stand-up skills + a cost model. This is the layer that turns the catalog into a micro/mini-SaaS factory. Index: patterns/stacks/registry.json. See docs/patterns-registry.md#stack-profiles.

profiles.summary: Firebase Hosting + Cloud Run + Firestore/Atlas + Firebase Auth + Stripe + Resend + Secret Manager + keyless WIF, as a T0->T1->T2 cost ladder ($0 PoC -> paid prod).

candidates.title: Bidirectional Reconciliation Sweep
candidates.summary: Periodic job repairing divergence between local records and an external system in both directions plus a stale-in-flight sweep. Fail-closed on unknown age, re-confirm before destructive action, idempotent guarded updates, per-run counts report. Its run reports and audit stream are operational-health signal.

candidates.title: Transactional Email & Dunning Lifecycle
candidates.summary: Idempotent, provider-neutral lifecycle messaging: welcome, activation nudge (fires if a signed-up user hasn't hit the activation event), re-engagement, and a failed-payment dunning sequence with bounded retries before degrade. Send-once keys prevent duplicate sends across retries. Recovery outcomes are conversion/retention signal.

candidates.title: Referral / Invite Growth Loop
candidates.summary: Invite -> signed invite token (server-trusted, single-use, expiring) -> attribution on accept -> reward both sides. Reuses the signed-token discipline; attribution is trust-tagged signal. A self-serve growth loop the improvement loop can optimize (reward size/copy) under admin gating.

candidates.title: Feature Entitlements Read-Model
candidates.summary: A single entitlement resolver deriving capability flags from tier + per-account overrides + grandfathering, consumed identically by backend gates and the frontend (one source of 'what can this account do'). Distinct from the tier matrix: it layers overrides and is the read model onboarding + UI both query.

candidates.title: Floor-Raising Entitlement Overlay (admin comps)
candidates.summary: An admin-granted plan/limits overlay stored LOCALLY and ORTHOGONALLY to the payer tier; effective limits = per-field max(payer, overlay), grant-only (never lowers a payer), and the billing reconcile recomputes the max so a webhook can never revert a comp. The load-bearing subtlety: the 'unlimited' sentinel (-1) must beat any finite value in BOTH directions or a naive numeric max silently lowers an unlimited plan. Enforcement reads EFFECTIVE limits, not the raw payer tier. Distinct from feature-entitlements: this is the narrower grant-only monotonic-floor invariant that survives an orthogonal source-of-truth reconcile. Chosen over Stripe coupons deliberately so bespoke custom limits (that coupons can't express) stay in-house. Realized in dogeared-coach.

invariants.statement: Floor-raising: effective(field) = max(base, overlay) per field; overlay only ever raises.
Grant-only writer: overlay path accepts only upward grants (paid tiers, reason required, audited), blocked from impersonated/suspended sessions.
Unlimited (-1 sentinel) beats finite in BOTH directions, or a naive max silently lowers an unlimited plan.
Orthogonal source of truth: payer tier persisted separately (StripePlan) from effective; reconcile recomputes max(payer, overlay) so a comp is never reverted.
Single enforcement seam reads EFFECTIVE limits, not the raw payer tier.

candidates.title: Self-Serve Billing Portal
candidates.summary: Let users manage plan, payment method, and seats without support -- provider-hosted portal session minted server-side + a local mirror of plan/seat state kept authoritative via billing webhooks. Removes the #1 support-ticket class for tiered SaaS.

candidates.title: Immutable Assignment Snapshot
candidates.summary: Pin the exact version of a composite item at PUBLISH time (edit -> new immutable published version; assignment references a version id, not the live template) so later edits never retroactively mutate outstanding assignments. The version snapshot is the liability boundary: write-once (Insert/Find only, no Update/Delete), item fields value-copied at publish. Keeps longitudinal completion analysis clean (no denominator drift). Nuance mined from code: immutability lives in the write-once version snapshot at publish, not a copy-into-the-assignment.

invariants.statement: Published version is write-once: no in-place patch (repo exposes Insert+Find only).
Editing creates a NEW version; outstanding assignments keep referencing the old immutable version id.
Composite-item fields are value-copied into the version at publish (snapshot), read from the pin at hydrate.

candidates.title: Language-Agnostic Build/Test Floor
candidates.summary: Auto-detecting build+test+lint gate across a polyglot repo, with a local pre-merge script mirroring CI; optionally zero-cost-until-opt-in (manual-dispatch trigger) for cost-sensitive private repos.

meta_disciplines.$comment: Recurring cross-pattern engineering rules worth stating once, not registry entries themselves.
rules: fail-closed on unknown/ambiguous input (grace windows, revocation lookups, tenant resolution, unknown-tier fallback)
idempotency + guarded conditional updates for anything touching external state (incl. send-once keys for messaging)
injected interface seams for every gate so services stay testable without real infra
document accepted TOCTOU/limitation classes inline rather than hiding them
external state changes are authoritative via signed webhooks, never the client redirect
